title: Image 3 of Vandalia Whig and Illinois intelligencer (Vandalia, Ill.), September 11, 1833
description: dings against the Mormons did not con tain all the facts Partridge their prin cipal leader and another member of the society were tarred and feathered We have seen no intimation of the future movements of this people Independence Mo July 21 1833 Sir1 have this day witnessed a new and stiangc thing Indignation has for sometime been raging against the Mor mons but more especially as they were gathering strength in numbers and seem ed disposed to increase the same by the Introduction of free people of color This day 400 men were assembled and under the direction of some of the princi pal characters their brick printing otBce was torn down to the ground the store hut up and Partridge their principal leader together with another man was tarred and feathered Cowdray the A postle McClellan the Preacher and Phelps the Printer made tneir escape Order was maintained through all lie proceedings of the day and every thing was done in as regular and temperate a manner as the nature of the case would permit but with a resolution not to be re sisted Yours respectfully SALMON SHERWOOD A CHAPTER ON HATS Your bonnet to its right useTis
